ops.title: supervise: Pool disposable-worker contract (nominate/corroborate Supervisor historical)

ops.body:
# Pool contract (current, 7623022)

supervise.Pool bounds concurrently running disposable worker processes (NewPool(limit, registry); both required; WorkerRegistry persists worker identities across daemon generations — *proc.Reaper implements it). The safety spine: each Task gets a FRESH process group, and its identity is durably tracked BEFORE the payload is delivered — Task.Stdin is withheld from the worker until the process-group identity is durable, so no crash window leaves an untracked worker already consuming input. Run executes one task and synchronously reaps its process; cancellation and deadlines terminate the ENTIRE worker process group before Run returns via the identity-gated ladder: group SIGTERM → TerminationGrace (fixed 500ms) → identity-revalidated SIGKILL.

- ErrUnsettledGroup: the durable group leader exited before the group could be identity-gated and terminated — the record is RETAINED for a later reap, never dropped.
- Close permanently stops admission; running tasks are unchanged. Cancel stops admission AND runs the termination ladder on every admitted task; call Wait to join.
- Wait's settlement rule: a caller deadline is reported only after every admitted worker has been reaped or proven no longer to own its record (identity-safe settlement — a deadline never abandons a live tracked group).
- Recover reaps durably tracked workers from prior daemon generations.
- supervise.Workers is the lifecycle contract Pool implements; daemon.RuntimeConfig declares an analogous Workers seam with the same rule ("Wait must not return while an admitted worker or tracked process group remains alive").

# Historical: the nominate/corroborate/act Supervisor (removed in 7623022)

The replaced in-process child Supervisor's fail-closed principles carry forward as rationale: a staleness signal only NOMINATED death and an authoritative Alive probe had to corroborate before any action; every probe error read as not-confirmed-dead; kill/respawn ran under the same per-subject lock as the consumer's manual-kill path (KeyedMutex) with status re-read under the lock so a manual kill was never double-actioned; the respawn strike was recorded BEFORE the respawn so a failing respawn still consumed budget; roster enumeration failure was "no signal", never "all subjects gone"; StartupGrace suppressed nomination for fresh spawns. Sliding-window strike/park budgets live on in proc/strikes.go and drain/run.go's Gate.
